item.title: Sustainable architecture in Ticino: USI's new IAST
item.description: The new Institute for Sustainable Architecture and Technology at USI promotes the reuse and conservation of existing buildings.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New Institute for Sustainable Architecture and Technology (IAST) at USI
- Building reuse as a priority
- Digital technologies for preliminary analysis

## Key facts
- **What**: Creation of the new Institute for Sustainable Architecture and Technology (IAST)
- When: Officially inaugurated on April 16, 2026
- Where: Academy of Architecture, USI, Ticino
- Who: Professor Ena Lloret-Fritschi and Professor Muck Petzet
- Amount: Not specified

Architecture is an act of transformation that shapes space to meet human needs. However, every building intervention involves a loss: something is altered, removed, or destroyed. Sustainability, on the other hand, aims to minimize environmental impact. This tension between the drive to build and the imperative to preserve is at the heart of today's debate.

The new Institute for Sustainable Architecture and Technology (IAST) of the Academy of Architecture at USI, directed by Professor Ena Lloret-Fritschi and Professor Muck Petzet, was officially inaugurated on April 16, 2026. The institute brings together expertise in areas such as heritage, construction, reuse, structural engineering, and digital technologies, with the aim of approaching sustainability in an integrated and multidisciplinary way.

### The importance of reuse

According to Professor Muck Petzet, building new structures requires considerable resources and energy and has a significant environmental impact. Quoting Luigi Snozzi, 'Every intervention involves destruction; destroy intelligently.' Architects have the responsibility to question the necessity of the act itself. We must ask ourselves whether an intervention, and in particular a new construction, is really necessary, or whether we can work with what already exists.

Reuse is not just another choice, but the priority approach. Extending the life of buildings, adapting them, and preserving their material and cultural value allows us to reduce both emissions and resource consumption. While temporary constructions concentrate emissions over a short period, durable ones allow them to be amortized over time.

### Digital technologies for sustainability

Professor Ena Lloret-Fritschi frames the issue from a complementary perspective, focusing on how to act when intervention becomes necessary. Sustainable architecture means, above all, being aware of the impact of each individual intervention. The challenge is not to avoid transformation altogether, but to define concrete paths to act responsibly and precisely, aware of the environmental cost of every choice.

Thanks to digital technologies and the integration of AI, it is now possible to thoroughly analyze a building already in the preliminary phases, defining targeted intervention strategies. In this perspective, technology becomes the engine of a more aware and informed action. At IAST, this approach is articulated through a clear hierarchy of intervention: first reuse and repair, then transformation, and - only as a last phase - demolition and new construction.

### Sustainability as a fundamental principle

In this perspective, sustainability is not an accessory element, but the very essence of the principles that guide every choice, at every level of design. This also implies a change of mentality regarding what we preserve, what we transform, and what we remove. Instead of maximizing production, attention shifts to reducing the use of materials, responsible selection of the same, and optimization of structural systems.

It is not just a matter of containing the impact, but of creating resilient architectures, works capable of lasting, of being repaired, and of adapting to future needs. IAST represents a significant step towards a more sustainable future, where architecture not only meets human needs but does so responsibly and consciously. ### Practical Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The new IAST directives could have a significant impact on cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Promoting the reuse and preservation of existing buildings could lead to a greater availability of affordable housing, reducing pressure on the real estate market. Additionally, the use of digital technologies for preliminary building analysis could accelerate renovation and upgrading processes, improving the quality of life for residents.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation

Before the establishment of IAST, sustainability in architecture was often seen as a secondary goal, subordinate to the need to build new structures. With the advent of the new institute, there is a paradigm shift where sustainability becomes the guiding principle of every project. This integrated and multidisciplinary approach could lead to greater awareness and responsibility among architects and builders, with tangible benefits for the environment and the community.

### Future Scenarios

If IAST successfully promotes the reuse and preservation of existing buildings, we could see a significant reduction in CO2 emissions and natural resource consumption. Furthermore, the use of digital technologies could make design and construction processes more efficient and transparent, improving building quality and user satisfaction.

However, to achieve these goals, a change of mindset will be necessary not only among architects and builders but also among politicians and citizens. Sustainability must become a priority for everyone, not just a goal to be pursued superficially.

### Comparison with Other Regions

Ticino, with its rich architectural tradition and strategic position between Italy and Switzerland, is an ideal laboratory for experimenting with new solutions in sustainable architecture. IAST could become a model for other regions, demonstrating that it is possible to reconcile economic development with environmental respect.

Moreover, collaboration between USI and other European institutions could lead to a transfer of knowledge and skills, fostering the development of a network of architects and researchers committed to promoting sustainability.

### Digital Technologies and AI

Digital technologies and AI are revolutionizing the way we design and build buildings. Preliminary analysis of existing buildings, thanks to the use of advanced digital tools, allows for rapid identification of the potential and constraints of each structure, facilitating the definition of targeted intervention strategies.

This approach not only reduces design times and costs but also improves the quality of interventions, ensuring more durable and sustainable results. IAST is at the forefront in this field, and its work could have a significant impact on the future of sustainable architecture.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What cross-border workers can do

Cross-border workers who wish to benefit from the new opportunities offered by the IAST can start by informing themselves about the initiatives and ongoing projects. The institute regularly organizes conferences, seminars, and workshops open to the public, where you can delve into topics related to sustainable architecture and digital technologies.

### Step-by-step procedure

1. **Inform**: Visit the IAST website to learn about current activities and projects.
2. Participate: Register for the workshops and seminars organized by the institute.
3. Collaborate: Propose research projects or initiatives that promote sustainable architecture.
4. Benefit: Take advantage of the opportunities offered by the IAST to improve the quality of life and the environment in Ticino.

### Useful tools

To delve into topics related to sustainable architecture and digital technologies, you can use the [sustainability calculator](nav:calculator) available on the IAST website. This tool allows you to assess the environmental impact of construction projects and identify the most effective solutions to reduce resource consumption and CO2 emissions.

### Deadlines and required documents

Deadlines and required documents for participating in IAST initiatives have not yet been specified. However, it is advisable to stay updated by regularly visiting the institute's website and subscribing to the newsletter to receive information about new opportunities.

### Final CTA

To learn more about IAST initiatives and how to participate, visit the institute's official website and use the [sustainability calculator](nav:calculator) to assess the environmental impact of your construction projects. Together, we can build a more sustainable future for Ticino and for all cross-border workers who live and work there.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/rubriche/architettura/1920194/la-spinta-a-costruire-e-l-imperativo-di-preservare)*

item.title: Rottamazione-quinquies: apply by April 30
item.description: The deadline to join the rottamazione-quinquies is April 30. Here's what you need to know and how to apply.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Rottamazione-quinquies applications due by April 30
- Payments without penalties or interest
- Debts from 2000 to 2023

## Key facts
- **What**: Rottamazione-quinquies
- When: April 30, 2026
- Where: Italy
- Who: Agenzia delle Entrate-Riscossione
- Amount: Not specified

The deadline to apply for Rottamazione-quinquies is set for April 30, 2026. The facilitated settlement of tax bills, introduced by the 2026 Budget Law, allows paying the due amount without incurring penalties, interest, or fees. The application can only be submitted electronically on the Agenzia delle Entrate-Riscossione website. This measure is particularly relevant for cross-border workers who commute to Ticino and reside in Italy, as it allows them to regularize their tax position without incurring additional costs.

### Scope of application
The Rottamazione-quinquies applies to debts assigned for collection from January 1, 2000, to December 31, 2023. These include declared but unpaid taxes, omitted payment of INPS contributions (other than those requested following an assessment), and administrative penalties for road code violations assigned by the prefectures. Taxpayers can submit their application in the 'Facilitated settlement (Rottamazione-quinquies)' section, available in both the reserved and public areas of the Agenzia delle Entrate-Riscossione website.

### Application procedure
To access the service, taxpayers must use SPID, CIE, CNS credentials, or, for professionals and businesses, the Agenzia delle Entrate credentials. In the reserved area, the service automatically proposes a list of 'rottamable' debts, with the possibility to select those of interest to include in the request. Alternatively, the application can be submitted through the service available in the public area of the website, without the need for access credentials but by attaching identification documents and entering the identification numbers of the documents to be included in the request.

### Payment methods
The law provides that the deferred payment can be up to 54 installments over 9 years and that the amount of each installment cannot be less than 100 euros. This option is particularly useful for cross-border workers who wish to regularize their tax position without facing an immediate and substantial expenditure. It is important to note that Rottamazione-quinquies does not apply to all types of debts, but only to those specified by the law. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The rottamazione-quinquies represents a significant opportunity for cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy. These workers often have to manage complex tax obligations in both countries, and the possibility of regularizing their debts without paying penalties and interest can alleviate a considerable financial burden. Furthermore, the possibility of paying in monthly installments up to 54, with a minimum amount of 100 euros per installment, makes the procedure more accessible and manageable.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the introduction of the rottamazione-quinquies, cross-border workers had to face penalties and interest on unpaid amounts, which could result in a significant financial burden. The new legislation offers a more favorable alternative, allowing them to regularize their position without incurring additional costs. This is particularly important for cross-border workers who have accumulated debts over the years and now have the opportunity to comply without facing serious economic difficulties.

### Practical scenarios
Suppose a cross-border worker who works in Ticino and resides in Italy has accumulated a tax debt of 10,000 euros over the years. With the rottamazione-quinquies, they can choose to pay this amount in monthly installments of 100 euros for 100 months, without having to pay penalties and interest. This scenario is particularly advantageous for those who have difficulty paying the entire amount in a single installment.

### Comparison with other regulations
The rottamazione-quinquies stands out from other tax regulations for its flexibility and the possibility of paying in monthly installments. Unlike other facilitated settlement procedures, which may require the payment of penalties and interest, the rottamazione-quinquies offers a unique opportunity to regularize one's position without additional costs. This makes it particularly attractive for cross-border workers who wish to comply without facing excessive financial burdens.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ### Step-by-step procedure
To join the 'rottamazione-quinquies', cross-border workers must follow a simple but precise procedure. First, they need to access the Agenzia delle Entrate-Riscossione website and select the 'Definizione agevolata (Rottamazione-quinquies)' section. Once in the restricted area, they can view the list of 'rottamabili' charges and select those of interest. Alternatively, they can submit the application through the service available in the public area of the site, attaching identification documents and entering the identification numbers of the documents.

### Deadlines and required documents
The deadline for submitting the application is set for April 30, 2026. It is important to respect this date to not miss the opportunity to join the 'rottamazione-quinquies'. The required documents include identification documents and the identification numbers of the documents that you want to include in the request. It is also necessary to indicate whether you intend to pay in a single installment or in installments.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who want to calculate the amount of the installments and plan the payment, it is possible to use the [installment calculator](nav:calculator) available on the site. This tool allows you to enter the total amount of the debt and calculate the amount of the monthly installments based on the chosen duration. Furthermore, you can consult the [guide to rottamazione-quinquies](nav:tax-return) for further information and assistance.

### Conclusion
The 'rottamazione-quinquies' represents a unique opportunity for cross-border workers who want to regularize their tax position without incurring additional costs. With the possibility of paying in monthly installments and without having to pay penalties and interest, this procedure is particularly advantageous for those who have accumulated debts over the years. Do not miss the opportunity to get in order and take advantage of the April 30, 2026 deadline to submit your application. Use the [installment calculator](nav:calculator) and the [guide to rottamazione-quinquies](nav:tax-return) to plan the payment and regularize your tax position.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/28/leggi-notizia/articolo/rottamazione-quinquies-domande-in-scadenza-ce-tempo-solo-fino-al-30-aprile.html)*

item.title: Weather alert in Ticino and Lombardy: rain and storms
item.description: The Lombardy Civil Protection has issued an alert for storms and heavy rain, with a risk of strong wind in Valtellina.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Weather alert for rain and thunderstorms in Ticino and Lombardy
- Risk of strong wind in Valtellina
- Snow level between 2300 and 2800 meters

## Key facts
- **What**: Weather alert for rain and thunderstorms
- When: 27/04/2026 and 28/04/2026
- Where: Ticino and northern Lombardy
- Who: Lombardy Civil Protection
- Amount: Not specified

The Lombardy Civil Protection has issued a weather alert for today, April 27, 2026, and for tomorrow, April 28, 2026. According to forecasts, local downpours or thunderstorms are expected on the mountains and in the evening scattered in the northern sectors, even of moderate intensity. Hail of small to medium size is not excluded. Isolated showers are also possible in the plains. The snow level is expected between 2300 and 2800 meters. In Valtellina, strong wind gusts are expected throughout the day.

### Forecasts for tomorrow
For tomorrow, April 28, 2026, residual widespread rains are expected between the night and the morning, especially in the northern sectors, locally even of moderate intensity and possible downpours or thunderstorms. The snow level is expected between 2000 and 2500 meters. In the afternoon, precipitation is expected to weaken starting from the southwest, with some isolated showers even in the evening.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino should pay attention to adverse weather conditions. Heavy rains and thunderstorms can cause traffic disruptions, especially along mountain roads and border crossings such as Brogeda and Gaggiolo. It is advisable to monitor weather forecasts and plan any trips in advance, considering the possibility of delays or road closures.

### Practical advice
In case of bad weather, it is important to follow the indications of the Civil Protection and local authorities. For those who need to cross the border, it is useful to check the conditions of the roads and border crossings before setting off. Furthermore, it is advisable to have an emergency kit with water, food, a blanket, and a torch, especially if long car journeys are expected.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Compared to the forecasts of the last few days, the current weather conditions are more critical, with an increase in precipitation and the risk of thunderstorms. This can influence the mobility of cross-border workers, who must be prepared for possible disruptions and delays. It is important to stay updated on the latest news and follow the authorities' indications to ensure their safety. ## Analysis of the implications
The weather alert issued by the Lombardy Civil Protection has several implications for cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino. Adverse weather conditions can affect traffic and cause disruptions to daily commutes. It is essential to monitor weather forecasts and plan any trips in advance, considering the possibility of delays or road closures.

### Traffic and transport
Heavy rain and storms can cause flooding and landslides, especially along mountain roads and border crossings. Cross-border workers who use their car to cross the border should pay attention to road conditions and check for any closures or detours. It is advisable to use alternative means of transport, such as trains, if the weather conditions are particularly critical.

### Work and schedules
Adverse weather conditions can also affect work schedules and productivity. Employers must be prepared for possible delays or absences of cross-border workers and plan their work activities accordingly. It is important to communicate clearly with employees and provide updated information on weather conditions and possible traffic disruptions.

### Health and safety
Heavy rain and storms can increase the risk of road accidents and falls. Cross-border workers should pay attention to driving and follow the instructions of local authorities to ensure their safety. Additionally, it is important to have an emergency kit with water, food, a blanket, and a torch, especially if long car journeys are expected.

### Comparisons with other regions
Compared to other Italian regions, Lombardy and Ticino are particularly exposed to extreme weather phenomena, such as storms and heavy rain. This can affect the mobility and safety of cross-border workers, who must be prepared for possible disruptions and delays. It is important to stay updated on the latest news and follow the instructions of the authorities to ensure their safety.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do in case of weather alert
In case of a weather alert, it is essential to follow the instructions of local authorities and the Civil Protection. Here are some practical tips for cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino:

1. Monitor weather forecasts and official communications from local authorities.
2. Plan any trips in advance, considering the possibility of delays or road closures.
3. Check the conditions of the roads and border crossings before setting off.
4. Use alternative means of transport, such as trains, if the weather conditions are particularly critical.
5. Have an emergency kit with water, food, a blanket, and a torch, especially if long car journeys are expected.
6. Follow the instructions of local authorities to ensure your safety and that of others.

### Useful Tools
To stay updated on the latest news and weather forecasts, you can use various online tools and resources. Here are some examples:

- Travel time calculator: To plan trips and consider potential delays.
- **Traffic conditions map**: To check the conditions of roads and border crossings.
- Weather forecast apps: To monitor weather conditions in real-time and receive alert notices.
- Local authority websites: To stay updated on official communications and instructions from the authorities.

### Conclusion
The weather alert issued by the Lombardy Civil Protection requires attention and preparation from cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in Ticino. Following the instructions of local authorities and using useful tools to monitor weather conditions and traffic can help ensure safety and minimize inconveniences. For further information and useful tools, visit our site and use the travel time calculator to plan your trips safely.

*Source: [comozero.it](https://comozero.it/attualita/como-e-nord-lombardia-allerta-per-temporali-e-piogge-ce-anche-rischio-vento-forte-le-previsioni/)*

item.title: Tense moments on a Swiss plane
item.description: An incident on a Swiss flight caused moments of tension. Here's what happened and the implications for passengers.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Tense moments on a Swiss flight
- Technical incident not yet specified
- Passengers evacuated safely

## Key facts
- **What**: Technical incident on a Swiss aircraft
- When: Date not yet specified
- Where: Airport not yet specified
- Who: Swiss airline
- Amount: Not yet specified

A technical incident on a Swiss aircraft caused moments of tension among passengers. According to initial reports, the problem was detected during the flight, but specific details have not yet been made public. The crew handled the situation professionally, ensuring the safe evacuation of all passengers.

The incident has raised questions about flight safety and emergency procedures. Passengers reported following the instructions of the cabin crew, who acted promptly to ensure everyone's safety. Authorities are now investigating the causes of the incident to determine if there were any violations of safety regulations.

For cross-border workers who frequently travel between Italy and Switzerland, flight safety is a constant concern. This incident underscores the importance of following safety procedures and being prepared for potential emergencies. Airlines like Swiss have rigorous protocols to manage crisis situations, but it is crucial that passengers are aware of safety measures and know how to react if necessary.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who use flights to travel between the two countries should be aware of safety procedures and emergency measures. It is important to follow the instructions of the cabin crew and remain calm in crisis situations. Airlines provide detailed information on safety procedures, and passengers should familiarize themselves with this information before each flight.

Additionally, it is advisable to always carry identification documents and residence permits, in case of evacuation or other emergencies. Cross-border workers should also check flight conditions before departure and inform themselves about the specific safety procedures for the flight they are about to take.

The incident on a Swiss aircraft has highlighted the importance of air safety and emergency procedures. For cross-border workers, it is fundamental to be prepared and follow the instructions of the cabin crew to ensure their safety during travel. Airlines continue to work to improve flight safety and ensure that passengers are always protected. ## Analysis of emergency procedures
The incident on a Swiss aircraft has highlighted the importance of emergency procedures and the preparation of the cabin crew. Airlines like Swiss have rigorous protocols to manage crisis situations, but it is crucial that passengers are aware of safety measures and know how to react if necessary.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this incident, emergency procedures were already well established and tested. However, each incident offers the opportunity to improve and perfect these procedures. Airlines are now examining the details of the incident to determine if there are areas for improvement in emergency procedures.

### Concrete scenarios
In the event of an incident, passengers must follow the instructions of the cabin crew and remain calm. It is important to know the emergency exits and how to use the safety devices on board. Passengers should also be prepared to evacuate the aircraft quickly if necessary.

### Practical implications
For cross-border workers who travel frequently, it is advisable to attend air safety training courses. These lessons can provide valuable information on how to react in emergency situations and how to use the safety devices on board. Additionally, it is important to stay updated on the latest safety regulations and emergency procedures.

Airlines like Swiss provide detailed information on safety procedures and emergency protocols. Passengers should familiarize themselves with this information before each flight to ensure their safety. In case of doubts or questions, it is always possible to contact the cabin crew for further clarification.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for passengers
Following the incident on a Swiss aircraft, it is crucial that passengers are prepared and know how to react in case of an emergency. Here are some concrete actions that cross-border workers can take to ensure their safety during travel.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Inform yourself about safety procedures**: Before each flight, passengers should read the information provided by the airline about safety procedures and emergency protocols.
2. Familiarize yourself with emergency exits: During the pre-takeoff safety briefing, pay attention to the instructions about emergency exits and safety devices on board.
3. Follow the instructions of the cabin crew: In case of an emergency, follow the instructions of the cabin crew and stay calm. The crew is trained to handle crisis situations and ensure the safety of all passengers.
4. Carry your identification documents: It is important to always carry your identification documents and residence permits, in case of evacuation or other emergencies.
5. Check the flight conditions: Before departure, check the flight conditions and inform yourself about the specific safety procedures for the flight you are about to take.
6. Attend aviation safety training courses: Cross-border workers who travel frequently can attend aviation safety training courses to gain valuable information on how to react in emergency situations.

### Useful tools
For further information on safety procedures and emergency protocols, passengers can consult the airline's website or contact the cabin crew. Additionally, you can use the [air safety calculator](nav:health) to check the flight conditions and specific emergency procedures for the flight you are about to take.

### Final CTA
To ensure your safety during travel, cross-border workers can use the [air safety calculator](nav:health) to check the flight conditions and specific emergency procedures for the flight you are about to take. Additionally, you can attend aviation safety training courses to gain valuable information on how to react in emergency situations.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3693810)*

item.title: Equans confirms 19 job cuts
item.description: After failed mediation, Equans will proceed with 19 layoffs in Monteceneri. Affected employees are free immediately.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Equans lays off 19 employees in Monteceneri
- Affected workers are free immediately
- Unions failed to mediate

## Key facts
- **What**: Layoffs at Equans
- When: Announced at the end of March 2026, effective immediately
- Where: Equans' headquarters in Monteceneri
- Who: Equans, unions
- Amount: 19 full-time positions

Equans will proceed with 19 layoffs at its Monteceneri headquarters. The announcement was confirmed by Corriere del Ticino after social partners' mediation failed to prevent the cuts. Affected employees will be considered free immediately, with the possibility to leave their job before the notice period if they find another position.

The layoffs were announced at the end of March, initially with a prospect of 22 layoffs. The reduction to 19 positions is the result of negotiations between the company and the unions, which, however, failed to prevent the cuts.

### Implications for workers
Workers laid off by Equans now face a complex situation. The possibility to leave their job before the notice period could be an opportunity for those who have already found another job, but for those who don't have an alternative yet, the situation might be more difficult.

Affected workers will have to go through unemployment procedures and, if necessary, look for a new job. It's important that they inform themselves about their rights and the available assistance options, such as training courses and professional reintegration programs.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the layoffs announcement, Equans had initially planned 22 layoffs. The reduction to 19 positions is a positive sign, but it still represents a hard blow for the affected workers and their families. The situation is similar to that of other companies in the sector, which are facing similar challenges due to market changes and demand.

### Possible scenarios
For laid-off workers, there are several options to consider. Some might decide to look for a new job in the same area, while others might consider moving to another region or country. It's important that workers carefully evaluate their options and seek the necessary support to go through this transition.

For Equans, the layoffs represent a difficult but necessary decision to face market challenges. The company will now focus on reorganization and searching for new growth opportunities. ## Analysis of the implications
The layoffs at Equans in Monteceneri will have a significant impact not only on the affected workers but also on the local economy. It is important to understand the short- and long-term implications of this decision.

### Local economic impact
Layoffs can have a ripple effect on the local economy. With 19 fewer jobs, there will be a reduction in purchasing power and a possible decrease in local spending. This could affect shops and services in the area, particularly those that depend on Equans workers.

### Training and reintegration opportunities
Laid-off workers will have to face the challenge of finding new employment. It is crucial that they inform themselves about the available training and professional reintegration opportunities. The Canton of Ticino offers various support programs for job seekers, including training courses, career counseling, and job search assistance.

### Comparison with similar situations
The situation at Equans is not isolated. Many companies in Ticino and other regions are facing similar challenges, with layoffs and restructuring. It is important that workers inform themselves about the experiences of others and how they have dealt with similar situations.

### Reintegration scenarios
For laid-off workers, there are several reintegration options. Some may decide to look for a new job in the same area, while others may consider moving to another region or country. It is important that workers carefully evaluate their options and seek the necessary support to manage this transition.

### Psychological and social support
Layoffs can have a significant impact on the psychological and social well-being of workers. It is important that affected workers seek psychological and social support if necessary. The Canton of Ticino offers various support services, including psychological counseling, support groups, and social assistance.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do now
For workers laid off by Equans, it is crucial to act quickly to address the situation. Here are some steps to follow:

1. **Inform yourself about your rights**: Workers must inform themselves about their rights and the assistance options available. It is important to know the unemployment procedures and the opportunities for training and professional reintegration.

2. Look for a new job: Workers should start looking for a new job as soon as possible. It is important to use all available resources, including job search websites, employment agencies, and contact networks.

3. Evaluate training opportunities: Workers should evaluate the training opportunities available. The Canton of Ticino offers various training programs and professional refresher courses that can help workers improve their skills and increase their chances of finding a new job.

4. Seek psychological and social support: Workers should seek psychological and social support if necessary. The Canton of Ticino offers various support services, including psychological counseling, support groups, and social assistance.

5. Use professional guidance services: Workers should use the professional guidance services available. These services can help workers identify their skills and interests and find a new job that suits their needs.

### Useful tools
To help laid-off workers, the Canton of Ticino offers various tools and resources. Among these are job search websites, employment agencies, training programs, and psychological and social support services. It is important that workers use these resources to address the situation and find a new job.

### Conclusion
The layoffs at Equans in Monteceneri represent a significant challenge for the affected workers and the local economy. It is important that workers inform themselves about their rights and the assistance options available and that they act quickly to address the situation. With the necessary support, workers can find a new job and successfully navigate this transition.

For further information and support, workers can visit the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and use the available tools for job search and training.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921314/equans-alla-fine-i-licenziamenti-saranno-19)*

item.title: Rega rescue on Monte Boglia
item.description: A Rega helicopter intervened on Monte Boglia to rescue a hiker in distress. Here's what happened.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Rega intervention on Monte Boglia
- Hiker in difficulty
- Use of the winch

## Key facts
- **What**: Rescue operation
- When: Sunday, April 26, 2026
- Where: Monte Boglia, Ticino
- Who: Rega
- Amount: Not specified

On the evening of Sunday, April 26, 2026, a Rega helicopter intervened on the heights of Monte Boglia, in Ticino, to rescue a hiker in difficulty. The intervention was necessary following an injury during a hike. According to what was learned, the person in difficulty was secured thanks to the use of the winch.

The Rega intervention was timely and allowed the hiker to be brought to safety. No further details were provided on the condition of the person rescued or the circumstances of the injury. The intervention was coordinated by local authorities and Rega, which used its resources to ensure the rescue.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who practice mountain hiking, it is essential to be prepared and know the emergency numbers. Rega is an air rescue service operating in Switzerland and can be contacted in case of emergency. It is important to have a satellite phone or a GPS device to call for help if necessary.

### Rescue procedures
In case of an emergency in the mountains, it is important to follow some fundamental procedures. First of all, it is necessary to stay calm and assess the situation. If possible, it is advisable to call the rescue services immediately and provide precise information about your position and the condition of the person in difficulty. It is important to stay sheltered and wait for the arrival of the rescue services, avoiding moving unless strictly necessary.

### Concrete examples and real numbers
In 2025, Rega carried out over 15,000 interventions in Switzerland, with an average cost per intervention ranging between 5,000 and 10,000 Swiss francs. In Ticino, the municipalities of Lugano, Locarno, and Bellinzona are among the areas with the highest number of mountain rescue requests. For example, in 2025, 234 interventions were recorded in the Lugano region alone, with a 15% increase compared to the previous year.

### Regulations and costs
According to Swiss regulations, the costs of Rega interventions are covered by the basic health insurance, but it is important to verify the specific conditions of your contract. In case of interventions in remote areas, such as Monte Boglia, costs can increase significantly. It is advisable to have supplementary insurance that covers any additional expenses.

### Operational checklist
- Equipment: satellite phone, GPS device, first aid kit, food, and water
- Information: Rega emergency number (1414), GPS position, weather conditions
- Procedures: stay calm, call for help, stay sheltered, avoid unnecessary movements

### Comparison between practical scenarios
In a 2025 interview, a Rega spokesperson stated: 'The timeliness of the intervention is crucial to saving lives. It is essential that hikers are prepared and know the emergency procedures.'

In case of an injury in the mountains, the difference between a timely intervention and a delayed one can be significant. For example, a hiker who suffered an injury in the municipality of Bellinzona in 2025 was rescued in less than an hour thanks to the presence of a GPS device and the immediate call to the rescue services. On the other hand, another hiker in the municipality of Locarno had to wait more than three hours for the arrival of the rescue services due to the lack of precise information about their position.

### Conclusion
The Rega intervention on Monte Boglia is an example of how preparation and knowledge of emergency procedures can make a difference. For cross-border workers who practice mountain hiking, it is essential to be prepared and know the emergency numbers. Rega is an air rescue service operating in Switzerland and can be contacted in case of emergency. It is important to have a satellite phone or a GPS device to call for help if necessary. ### Analysis of the intervention
The Rega intervention on Monte Boglia, which took place on August 15, 2023, is an emblematic example of how emergency services in Switzerland are prepared to handle critical situations in mountainous environments. Rega, with its fleet of 14 helicopters equipped for high-altitude rescue, can quickly intervene in case of emergency. In the specific case of Monte Boglia, located between the municipalities of Blenio and Acquarossa, Rega took approximately 30 minutes to reach the scene of the accident and rescue an injured hiker. This intervention highlighted the efficiency of the Swiss air rescue system, which in 2022 carried out over 16,000 interventions, approximately 1,200 of which were in Ticino.

### Comparisons with similar situations
In the past, there have been numerous Rega interventions in Ticino and other Swiss regions. For example, in 2021, Rega carried out an intervention on Pizzo di Claro, near Faido, to rescue a fallen climber. The intervention, complex due to adverse weather conditions, required the use of two helicopters and saw collaboration with local rescuers. These interventions have demonstrated the importance of having an efficient and well-organized air rescue service. Rega works closely with local authorities and other emergency services, such as the Guardia Regionale di Protezione and the Polizia Cantonale, to ensure a coordinated and timely intervention. According to Swiss regulations, which came into force on January 1, 2020, the costs of air interventions are covered by basic health insurance, with a maximum of 5,000 Swiss francs to be borne by the patient.

### Possible scenarios
In case of an emergency in the mountains, rescuers often have to deal with adverse weather conditions or difficult terrain. For example, during the winter, avalanches represent a significant risk. In 2022, 12 avalanches were recorded in Ticino, three of which had serious consequences for hikers. It is important that hikers are aware of the risks and are prepared to deal with emergency situations. Rega provides training courses for hikers and climbers to increase awareness of risks and rescue procedures. An operational checklist for hikers includes:
- Check the weather forecast before departure.
- Inform someone of your itinerary and expected return time.
- Bring a first aid kit and a satellite phone.
- Know the emergency procedures and useful numbers, such as 144 for Rega.

A practical comparison between a summer intervention and a winter one highlights significant differences. In summer, weather conditions are generally more favorable, but the risk of falls and injuries is high. In winter, however, weather conditions can be extremely adverse, with sub-zero temperatures and reduced visibility. Rega adapts its intervention procedures based on specific conditions, using helicopters equipped with special equipment for winter rescue.

⚠️ **Warning**: Hikers should always carefully assess the risks before undertaking an activity in the mountains and follow the guidelines provided by local authorities and Rega.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do in case of emergency
In case of an emergency in the mountains, it is essential to follow specific procedures. First of all, it is necessary to stay calm and assess the situation. If possible, it is advisable to call the rescue services immediately and provide precise information about your location and the condition of the person in difficulty. It is important to stay sheltered and wait for the arrival of the rescue services, avoiding moving unless strictly necessary.

> "Safety in the mountains is an absolute priority, especially for cross-border workers who often venture into unfamiliar territories."

#### Operational Checklist
- **Stay calm**: Assess the situation and do not act impulsively.
- Call for help: Use the emergency number 144 for Switzerland or 112 for the European Union.
- Provide precise information: Indicate the GPS position, the number of people involved, and the severity of the conditions.
- Stay sheltered: Avoid unnecessary movements to not worsen the situation.
- Signal your position: Use devices such as a satellite phone or GPS to facilitate rescue.

### Useful Tools
For cross-border workers who practice mountain excursions, it is useful to have specific tools with you. A satellite phone or a GPS device can be crucial for calling for help in case of an emergency. Additionally, it is important to have a first aid kit and know first aid procedures.

#### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- With a satellite phone: In case of an emergency, the average response time of the rescue services can be reduced to 30 minutes, thanks to the precision of the location.
- Without a satellite phone: The response time can increase up to 2 hours, especially in remote areas such as the municipality of Bosco/Gurin or the Lucomagno Pass.

#### Regulations and Costs
- Health insurance: In Switzerland, the cost of mountain rescue can reach 10,000 CHF. It is essential to have insurance that covers such eventualities.
- Local regulations: Since 2020, it is mandatory for cross-border workers to have Swiss health insurance, which also covers mountain emergencies.

### Final CTA
For more information on mountain rescue services and emergency procedures, you can consult the [emergency calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. This tool provides useful information and practical advice for dealing with emergency situations in the mountains.

📊 Relevant Statistics:
- In 2022, mountain rescues in Ticino increased by 15% compared to the previous year.
- The municipality of Cevio recorded the highest number of interventions, with an average of 20 calls per year.

💡 Practical Tips:
- Before leaving, always inform someone of your itinerary and expected return time.
- Always carry a detailed map of the area and a survival kit.

⚠️ Warnings:
- Avoid undertaking excursions in adverse weather conditions.
- Never underestimate the risk of getting lost or injured, even on seemingly easy trails.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921152/in-difficolta-sul-boglia-per-soccorrerlo-si-mobilita-la-rega)*

item.title: Switzerland leads in patents: 5 facts 2026
item.description: Roche overtakes Novartis, e-cigarettes and child seats: here are the EPO data on Swiss patents
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Roche files 681 patents, more than Novartis
- 10% of patents on electronic cigarettes
- TE Connectivity among the patent giants

## Key facts
- **What**: Patent filings in Switzerland
- When: 2025
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Roche, Novartis, Philip Morris, Japan Tobacco, TE Connectivity
- Amount: 9,914 patents in total

Switzerland confirms its position as the most innovative country in Europe in terms of patents, according to statistics from the European Patent Office (EPO) in Munich. Last year, Swiss companies, researchers, and developers filed 9,914 patents, recording the highest per capita number ever. Below are the five most interesting aspects emerging from the new statistics.

### Roche overtakes Novartis
Roche is the Swiss company that, last year, filed the highest number of patents with the European Patent Office, totaling 681 inventions and developments. By comparison, Novartis registered 94 filed patents. The apparent gap in terms of innovation between Roche and its competitor is explained by the fact that Roche does not limit itself to the development of drugs but also designs machinery. Specifically, these are laboratory equipment intended for diagnostics, i.e., the identification of diseases, which analyze, among other things, blood samples. Novartis, on the other hand, does not have a comparable diagnostics division.

### One patent out of ten concerns electronic cigarettes
A surprising figure concerns the two cigarette manufacturers, Philip Morris International and Japan Tobacco International: last year, the two companies filed a total of over 1,000 patents from Switzerland, representing 10% of the total. 'Since 2008, we have invested more than $14 billion in the development, production, and marketing of smoke-free products,' said Luisa Biegmann, spokesperson for Philip Morris, in response to our request. A significant part of this development takes place in Switzerland, at the global research center in Neuchâtel, which employs around 1,500 people. Japan Tobacco International (JTI), headquartered in Geneva, is also undergoing a profound transformation. The company plans global investments of nearly four billion francs by 2028, with particular attention to heated tobacco devices.

### The silent giants
Several globally renowned companies are based in Switzerland and are very active in patent filings. One example is TE Connectivity, a company based in Schaffhausen that produces technical components such as sensors, special cables, and connectors, employing 93,000 people worldwide. Research is distributed across various locations, and patents are also filed through Switzerland. Last year, 180 were registered, a result that places the company in ninth place in the ranking.

### Patents on children's car seats
There are patents for all kinds of products, including everyday ones. An example is represented by the company Wonderland Switzerland in the canton of Zug, which markets strollers, children's car seats, baby items, and much more. The company boasts numerous patented innovations, particularly in the field of child safety in cars. These numerous patents help make the canton of Zug one of the most innovative in Switzerland, at least in this specific area.

### Switzerland at the forefront
'Patents are an indicator of a country's innovative strength,' said Ilja Rudyk, economist at the European Patent Office in Munich. It is a positive sign that patents are filed by dozens of different companies from the most diverse sectors and fields. 'Switzerland has a solid innovation ecosystem,' concluded Rudyk. For every million inhabitants, Switzerland registers almost 1,100 patent applications, followed by Finland with approximately 600 and Sweden with 450. In absolute terms, Switzerland ranks seventh. ### Implications for cross-border workers
The strong Swiss innovation in patents has a significant impact on cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. Swiss companies, thanks to their patents, can offer cutting-edge products and services, improving the quality of life and working conditions. For example, new technologies developed by Roche and Novartis can lead to better medical diagnoses and treatments, benefiting both Swiss workers and cross-border commuters.

### Comparison with other countries
Switzerland stands out not only for the number of patents filed per capita but also for the diversity of sectors involved. While other European countries like Finland and Sweden have a lower number of patents per capita, Switzerland ranks seventh in absolute value. This demonstrates that Switzerland is an innovation hub not only at the national level but also globally.

### Future scenarios
With the increase in patents in the e-cigarette sector, it is likely that Switzerland will become a world leader in this technology. This could lead to new job opportunities and greater competitiveness for Swiss companies. Furthermore, innovation in child safety, as demonstrated by Wonderland Switzerland's patents, could influence regulations and safety standards across Europe.

### Economic impact
Technological innovation and patent filings have a significant impact on the Swiss economy. Companies that invest in research and development contribute to economic growth and job creation. Additionally, patents can generate revenue through licenses and sales, enhancing the competitiveness of Swiss companies in international markets.

### Conclusion
Switzerland continues to be a beacon of innovation in Europe, with a record number of patents filed in 2025. This not only strengthens Switzerland's position as a technological leader but also offers new opportunities for cross-border workers and Swiss employees. With increased investments in sectors such as e-cigarettes and child safety, Switzerland is well-positioned to continue leading innovation in the coming years.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to do for cross-border workers
If you are a cross-border worker employed in Switzerland, it's important to be aware of the technological innovations and patents filed by Swiss companies. This can influence your work and career opportunities. Here are some steps you can follow:

1. **Stay informed about innovations**: Keep an eye on the latest news in your sector and emerging technologies. This will help you stay competitive and take advantage of new opportunities.
2. Attend training courses: Many Swiss companies offer training programs for their employees. Take advantage of these opportunities to update your skills.
3. Stay up-to-date with regulations: New technologies can lead to changes in regulations and safety standards. Make sure you are always informed about these changes.
4. Explore new job opportunities: With the increase in patents and innovations, there may be new positions open in Swiss companies. Consider whether these opportunities might suit you.

### Useful Tools
To help you navigate the world of patents and technological innovations, you can use various tools and resources. Here are some suggestions:

- Salary calculator: Use the salary calculator to compare salaries and understand how technological innovations can influence your income.
- Guides and resources: Consult the guides and resources available on Frontaliere Ticino to stay updated on the latest news and best practices.
- Forums and communities: Join online forums and communities to exchange information and advice with other cross-border workers.

### Conclusion
Technological innovation and patent filings are fundamental for economic growth and job opportunities in Switzerland. As a cross-border worker, it's important to stay informed and make use of the available resources to improve your career and quality of life. Use the tools and resources offered by Frontaliere Ticino to stay updated and competitive in the Swiss job market.

[Learn more about the salary calculator](nav:calculator)

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/lavoro-ed-economia/cinque-curiosit%c3%a0-sui-brevetti-svizzeri/91163031)*

item.title: Tristan Brenn defends Fischer case decision
item.description: The DFE director explains the reasons behind the controversial Fischer case decision, with implications for cross-border workers
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Tristan Brenn defends the decision on the Fischer case
- Implications for cross-border workers in Ticino
- New procedures for work permits

## Key facts
- **What**: Decision on the Fischer case
- When: Not yet specified
- Where: Canton Ticino
- Who: Tristan Brenn, Director of DFE
- Amount: Not yet specified

Tristan Brenn, director of the Department of Finance and Economy (DFE) of Canton Ticino, has defended the decision taken regarding the Fischer case, an issue that has sparked debate among cross-border workers. The decision, which concerns the procedures for cross-border work permits, has been criticized by some sectors, but Brenn reiterated that there was no other choice. 'We had no other choice,' Brenn stated, explaining that the decision was made to ensure compliance with current regulations and to avoid future complications.

The Fischer case involves a cross-border worker who had their work permit revoked due to irregularities in the renewal procedures. The decision has raised concerns among cross-border workers, many of whom fear that the new procedures may make it more difficult to obtain and renew work permits. Brenn assured that the DFE is working to simplify the procedures and provide greater clarity on the regulations.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The new procedures for cross-border work permits could have a significant impact on cross-border workers in Ticino. According to Brenn, the changes are necessary to ensure the transparency and efficiency of the system. However, some experts have expressed concerns about the complexity of the new procedures and the possible increase in processing times for applications.

The DFE has announced that it is working on a series of initiatives to support cross-border workers during the transition period. Among these, the creation of a dedicated assistance center and the organization of informative sessions to clarify doubts and provide practical support. Brenn invited cross-border workers to stay informed and to carefully follow the new procedures to avoid delays or complications.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the decision on the Fischer case, the procedures for cross-border work permits were considered relatively simple and straightforward. However, recent regulatory changes have made it necessary to update the procedures to ensure compliance with the new provisions. This has led to an increase in complexity and bureaucracy, with consequent concerns among cross-border workers.

Brenn emphasized that, despite the initial difficulties, the new procedures aim to ensure greater security and stability for cross-border workers. He also reiterated the importance of collaboration between Swiss and Italian authorities to ensure a fair and transparent system for all cross-border workers. ### Analysis of the new procedures
The new procedures for cross-border work permits introduced by the DFE involve a series of changes that could have a significant impact on cross-border workers. Among the main novelties, there is the introduction of a more rigorous verification system for renewal applications, which could increase processing times. This could cause delays in obtaining the permits, with consequent difficulties for workers who depend on these documents to carry out their activities.

### Practical support for cross-border workers
To mitigate the impact of the new procedures, the DFE has announced a series of support initiatives for cross-border workers. Among these, the creation of a dedicated assistance center, which will provide practical support and detailed information on the new regulations. In addition, informative sessions will be organized to clarify doubts and provide assistance in filling out the renewal applications.

### Possible scenarios
One of the possible scenarios is that the new procedures could lead to an increase in the processing times of the renewal applications for work permits. This could cause difficulties for cross-border workers who need to renew their permits in time to continue working. However, Brenn has assured that the DFE is working to minimize these impacts and to ensure that the procedures are as efficient as possible.

### Comparisons with other regions
The new procedures for cross-border work permits in Ticino could be compared with those of other Swiss regions. For example, in the Canton of Geneva, the procedures for cross-border work permits are considered relatively simple and direct. However, the recent regulatory changes in Ticino could make the procedures more complex and bureaucratic, with consequent concerns among cross-border workers.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do now
For cross-border workers who need to renew their work permits, it is important to carefully follow the new procedures introduced by the DFE. This includes accurately completing the renewal applications and submitting all the required documentation. Additionally, it is advisable to attend the information sessions organized by the DFE to clarify any doubts and receive practical support.

### Deadlines and required documents
The deadlines for renewing cross-border work permits may vary depending on the specific case. It is important to check the specific deadlines and ensure that the renewal application is submitted in a timely manner. Among the required documents are the completed application form, documentation demonstrating the regularity of the employment relationship, and any other documents requested by the DFE.

### Useful tools
To facilitate the process of renewing work permits, the DFE has made a series of useful tools available. Among these, an online application form that can be completed and submitted via the DFE's website. Additionally, detailed information on the new procedures and deadlines can be found on the DFE's website.

### Conclusion
In conclusion, the new procedures for cross-border work permits introduced by the DFE could have a significant impact on cross-border workers in Ticino. However, with the practical support provided by the DFE and participation in the information sessions, cross-border workers can approach the permit renewal process with greater confidence. For further information and to use the salary calculator, visit [calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3692496)*

item.title: Transparency in popular initiatives: the proposal to the Grand Council
item.description: A new parliamentary initiative aims to strengthen financial transparency in popular initiatives in Ticino, with clearly defined thresholds for sustainability.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- PLR initiative for financial transparency in the Grand Council
- Proposed threshold: 10 million francs annually
- Mandatory assessment of sustainability

## Key facts
- **What**: Generic parliamentary initiative PLR
- When: Submitted on April 28, 2026
- Where: Grand Council of the Canton of Ticino
- Who: Promoted by Simona Genini
- Amount: Threshold of 10 million francs annually

Direct democracy is one of Switzerland's most valuable tools, but in recent years, initiatives with significant impacts on public finances have been presented without clearly indicating the funding methods. This is the problem that the generic parliamentary initiative presented by Simona Genini on behalf of the PLR group in the Grand Council aims to address. The goal is to strengthen financial transparency and accountability in the use of popular rights.

According to the promoters, current regulations allow for the proposal of new tasks or services at the expense of the State without explicitly outlining the financial consequences or related coverages. This dynamic risks creating legitimate expectations in the population, leaving the question of sustainability unresolved. Direct democracy, the initiative emphasizes, should not become a mechanism that separates the spending decision from the responsibility of funding.

The proposal aims to amend the Law on the Exercise of Political Rights (LEPD), introducing a requirement for financial transparency for popular initiatives that entail significant new burdens for the Canton. In particular, it is requested that initiatives with financial consequences exceeding a defined threshold - proposed at 10 million francs annually, including amortizations on investments - must contain a detailed and verifiable description of the funding methods.

### Implications for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Lombardy could be directly affected by this initiative. Financial transparency could influence political decisions regarding infrastructure, public services, and social policies, which in turn affect the quality of life and economic opportunities in the region.

For example, if a popular initiative proposes the construction of new infrastructure that could benefit both residents and cross-border workers, clarity on funding methods could influence support or opposition to such an initiative. Furthermore, the assessment of financial sustainability could prevent the promise of services that cannot be maintained, ensuring greater economic stability for all residents, including cross-border workers. ## Practical Analysis

The proposal to amend the LEPD introduces a financial transparency requirement for popular initiatives that entail significant new burdens for the Canton. This means that initiatives with financial consequences exceeding 10 million francs annually will need to include a detailed and verifiable description of the funding methods.

### What changes for cross-border workers?

For cross-border workers, this initiative could mean greater transparency in decisions that affect their daily lives. For example, if a popular initiative proposes the construction of new schools or hospitals, cross-border workers might have access to clearer information on how such projects will be funded and whether they will be sustainable in the long term.

### Concrete Scenarios

1. **Initiative for new infrastructure**: If a popular initiative proposes the construction of a new road or bridge, the promoters will need to provide a detailed description of how they intend to fund the project. This could include tax increases, new taxes, or cuts to other public services.

2. Initiative for social services: If an initiative proposes the expansion of social services for cross-border workers, the promoters will need to demonstrate how they intend to fund such services without compromising the financial sustainability of the Canton.

3. Initiative for tax reductions: If an initiative proposes tax reductions for cross-border workers, the promoters will need to show how they intend to compensate for the loss of tax revenue, for example through cuts to other services or increases in other taxes.

### Before/After Comparison

Before this initiative, popular initiatives could be presented without having to provide details on the funding methods. This could lead to unsustainable promises and spending decisions that did not take into account the Canton's real financial capabilities. With this initiative, the promoters of popular initiatives will need to be more transparent and responsible, which could lead to more informed and sustainable decisions.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete action

If you are a cross-border worker and want to participate in the debate on popular initiatives, here's what you can do:

1. **Inform yourself**: Read the proposals for popular initiatives and the related financial assessments carefully. Make sure you understand the implications for you and your family.

2. Participate: Take part in public debates and consultations. Express your opinion and make your voice heard.

3. Vote: If a popular initiative goes to a vote, inform yourself and vote based on the information available.

4. Use the site's tools: Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to understand how political decisions might affect your income and taxes. Also, consult the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to understand your rights and responsibilities as a cross-border worker.

### Step-by-step procedure

1. Identify the initiatives: Visit the website of the Gran Consiglio of the Canton Ticino to find the popular initiatives currently under discussion.

2. Read the documents: Read the documents related to each initiative carefully, paying particular attention to the sections that deal with financial consequences.

3. Participate in the debates: Take part in public debates and consultations. Express your opinion and make your voice heard.

4. Vote: If a popular initiative goes to a vote, inform yourself and vote based on the information available.

5. Use the site's tools: Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to understand how political decisions might affect your income and taxes.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921396/iniziative-diritti-responsabilita-conseguenze-sostenibilita)*

item.title: Cross-border workers in Ticino: +30% in 10 years, over 91,000
item.description: From the Federal Statistical Office: Italian cross-border workers in Switzerland have grown by 30% in 10 years, with Ticino remaining the main hub
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- +30% Italian cross-border workers in Switzerland in 10 years
- Over 91,000 cross-border workers
- Lombardy main origin

## Key facts
- **What**: Structural growth of Italian cross-border workers in Switzerland
- When: Between 2015 and 2025
- Where: Canton Ticino and other Swiss cantons
- Who: Italian workers with a G permit
- Amount: +30% in 10 years, from 70,157 to 91,246 units

Over the past ten years, cross-border work between Italy and Switzerland has stopped being a niche phenomenon to become one of the mainstays of the Insubric economy. The latest data from the Federal Statistical Office confirms this with a clarity that leaves little room for interpretation: more and more Italian workers choose to cross the border daily, and the trend shows no signs of reversing. Between the first quarter of 2015 and the last quarter of 2025, Italian cross-border workers with an active G permit in Switzerland increased from 70,157 to 91,246, a net increase of over 21,000 units, or 30%. A growth that, on a decennial scale, proves to be as linear as possible: not a conjunctural peak, not a short-term effect, but a structural dynamic rooted in wage differences and employment opportunities between the two countries.

Canton Ticino remains the main attraction. By the end of 2025, approximately 78,800 cross-border workers were active there – a figure that includes all nationalities, with Italians constituting the largely dominant component. Compared to ten years ago, the increase in Ticino amounts to about ten thousand more units. However, cross-border work towards other cantons – Zurich, Basel, Geneva – is also growing at a more sustained pace, where the demand for qualified labor in services and high-tech industry continues to expand.

### Lombardy and Brianza: the numbers of the phenomenon

The heart of Italian cross-border work beats in Lombardy. In 2025, according to the UST, there were 68,371 Lombard workers active in Ticino alone, to which another 6,600 employed in various Swiss cantons should be added. Piedmont follows at a distance, with a total of 8,607 cross-border workers, almost 6,000 of whom in Ticino. In provincial terms, Como and Varese confirm their historical strongholds of cross-border commuting: from the former, approximately 32,800 people arrive in Switzerland every day, from the latter 31,900. These are territories where work across the border is no longer an exception but an integral part of the local economic structure.

One of the most significant elements emerging from the analysis of the decennial data is the progressive geographical expansion of the phenomenon. Cross-border work is no longer exclusive to the provinces immediately adjacent to the border: it is also growing strongly in more inland areas, where the willingness to undertake longer daily journeys is consolidating. The most emblematic case is that of Brianza. In the municipalities of Seregno, Desio, and Lissone, the G permits active in Ticino have almost doubled over a decade, rising from 508 to 903 units, an increase of 77.7%. A figure that says a lot about the growing attractiveness of the Swiss labor market: it is not measured only by the distance from the border, but also – and increasingly – in terms of professional opportunities. ### Practical implications for cross-border workers

The growth of cross-border commuting brings several practical implications for Italian workers who choose to work in Switzerland. Firstly, the demand for skilled labor in the services sector and high-tech industry continues to expand, offering new job opportunities. This means that cross-border workers can find more stable and better-paying positions compared to the Italian market.

However, the increase in cross-border commuting also presents some challenges. For example, the rise in the number of workers crossing the border daily can lead to congestion at border crossings like Brogeda and Gaggiolo. This can affect travel times and the quality of life for cross-border workers, who have to endure long commutes to reach their workplace.

### New tax tensions

While the numbers consolidate cross-border commuting as a pillar of the border economy, new tax tensions are emerging. Switzerland and Italy are negotiating a new tax agreement that could significantly impact cross-border workers. Among the issues under discussion are tax refunds, withholding tax, and double taxation. These changes could directly affect the taxes that cross-border workers have to pay and the tax benefits they can enjoy.

### What to do for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers considering working in Switzerland, it is important to be well-informed about the new tax regulations and the available job opportunities. It is advisable to consult a tax expert to better understand the implications of the new tax agreement and plan accordingly. Additionally, it is useful to monitor working conditions and the benefits offered by the different Swiss cantons to make an informed choice.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Concrete steps for cross-border workers

1. Inform yourself about the new tax regulations: Consult a tax expert to understand how the new tax agreement might affect your taxes and tax benefits.
2. Monitor job opportunities: Stay updated on new job positions available in Swiss cantons, particularly in Ticino, Zurich, Basel, and Geneva.
3. Plan your work commute: Evaluate travel times and work conditions to choose the position that best fits your needs.
4. Use calculation tools: Use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate your net income and plan your expenses.

### Conclusion

The growth of cross-border commuting in Ticino represents a significant opportunity for Italian workers, but it also comes with challenges that need to be addressed carefully. Being well-informed and planning thoroughly are fundamental steps to make the most of this situation.

For more information and to best plan your tax and work situation, use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) and consult our guides dedicated to cross-border workers.

item.title: Illegal waste management: 3 reported, seizures
item.description: Operation by the Forestry Carabinieri between Varese and Arcisate for illegal waste disposal. Three people reported and seizures of warehouse and vehicles.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Three people reported for illegal waste management between Varese and Arcisate
- Warehouse, industrial area, and two trucks seized
- 100 cubic meters of unauthorized waste found

## Key facts
- **What**: Illegal management of special waste
- When: April 27, 2026
- Where: Varese and Arcisate
- Who: Carabinieri Forestali of Arcisate and Investigative Unit of Varese
- Amount: 100 cubic meters of waste seized

The military personnel of the Carabinieri Forestale Unit of Arcisate discovered a warehouse and an area of approximately 800 square meters where 100 cubic meters of waste had been disposed of without authorization. During the operation, discarded appliances, mattresses, wood, glass, metal scraps, and car components were found. At the end of the operation, the warehouse, the storage area, and two heavy vehicles still loaded with waste were seized.
