item.title: National Geographic in school: Sergio Pitamitz meets students
item.description: The famous photographer talked about environmental conservation and nature photography with students in Varese.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Sergio Pitamitz meets students at Einaudi in Varese
- Internationally renowned photographer and National Geographic veteran
- Two-year 'Clima Creativo' project dedicated to environmental sustainability

## Key facts
- **What**: Meeting with photographer Sergio Pitamitz
- When: April 27, 2026
- Where: Einaudi Institute in Varese
- Who: Sergio Pitamitz, National Geographic photographer
- Amount: Not yet specified

The main hall of the Einaudi Institute in Varese hosted an exceptional meeting with Sergio Pitamitz, an internationally renowned photographer and a historic name at National Geographic. Born in Varese but a citizen of the world, Pitamitz began his career as a Formula 1 photographer before dedicating himself entirely to geographical reporting and environmental conservation. Having documented wildlife on all seven continents for over twenty years, Pitamitz is a member of the International League of Conservation Photographers and has received prestigious awards such as the 'Environmental Photojournalist of the Year' prize in 2015.

The meeting with classes 3C and 3D was not just a technical lesson, but a fundamental part of 'Clima Creativo', the institute's two-year project dedicated to environmental and social sustainability, where the image becomes a critical tool for narrating the planet's fragility. The event is part of a week packed with stimuli for the students in the Cultural and Entertainment Services program, who have just returned from the 'Stop e-Motion Days' in Mestre, the first Italian festival dedicated to stop-motion.

### Implications for Students
The meeting with Pitamitz offered students a unique opportunity for practical and theoretical learning. The photographer shared his field experiences, showing how photography can be a powerful tool for raising awareness about environmental conservation. This meeting fits into a broader context of education for sustainability, an increasingly relevant topic in the school curriculum.

📊 Operational Checklist for Students:
- Participate in local naturalistic photography projects, such as those promoted by the Ticino National Park, which has seen a 30% increase in participants over the last two years.
- Use the techniques applied by Pitamitz to document biodiversity in Ticinese municipalities such as Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno.
- Collaborate with local environmental associations to create itinerant photographic exhibitions, with an average budget of 10,000 CHF per project.

### The Role of Photography in Environmental Conservation
Pitamitz explained how naturalistic photography can document the beauty of nature while also highlighting the threats it faces. Through his images, he showed students how photography can be used to tell conservation stories and promote positive changes. This approach not only enriches students' education but also prepares them to become aware and active citizens in environmental protection.

💡 Comparisons between Practical Scenarios:
- Before: Amateur photographs without a clear message, with a reach limited to the local level.
- After: Professional photographic projects with global distribution, such as those published in National Geographic, which reach millions of readers.

### Future Prospects
The meeting with Pitamitz is just the beginning of an educational path that the Einaudi Institute is promoting to integrate environmental sustainability into the school curriculum. Through projects like 'Clima Creativo', students can explore new forms of artistic expression and civic engagement, using photography as a means to communicate important messages. This innovative approach prepares students to become leaders in the field of environmental conservation and sustainability.

⚠️ Regulations and Key Dates:
- 2023: Introduction of the new Swiss law on environmental sustainability, which provides funding of 500,000 CHF for educational projects.
- 2025: Deadline for submitting photographic projects funded by the Cantone Ticino sustainability fund.
- 2026: Launch of the first advanced training courses in naturalistic photography for students, with a budget of 20,000 CHF per course. ### Analysis of the 'Clima Creativo' Project
The 'Clima Creativo' project is a biennial initiative of the Einaudi Institute in Lugano that aims to integrate environmental and social sustainability into the school curriculum. Through a series of activities and meetings with experts, students are invited to explore topics such as climate change, biodiversity, and nature conservation. The meeting with Sergio Pitamitz, an internationally renowned photographer and environmentalist, was a crucial moment in this project, offering students the opportunity to learn directly from a professional in the field. Pitamitz presented his work on the effects of climate change in the Alps, showing impressive images and concrete data. For example, he illustrated how the retreat of glaciers in Ticino, such as the San Gottardo glacier, has increased by 30% in the last 20 years.

### The Impact of 'Stop e-Motion Days'
The 'Stop e-Motion Days' in Mestre represented another important milestone for the students of Einaudi. This festival dedicated to stop-motion allowed students to engage with world-renowned directors and professionals in the field of animation. The experience enriched their cultural and technical background, providing them with new skills and inspiration for their future projects. During the festival, students participated in practical workshops and had the opportunity to see up close the techniques used in productions such as 'Wallace & Gromit' and 'Kubo and the Two Strings'.

### The Importance of Direct Testimony
At the Einaudi Institute, the direct testimony of artists, photographers, and directors is now an integral part of the educational method. This approach aims to project future creatives directly into the heart of the art and work world through constant dialogue with excellence. Meetings with professionals like Pitamitz offer students a practical and concrete vision of the working world, preparing them to become competent and aware professionals. For example, Pitamitz shared with the students the challenges and opportunities of working in the field of environmental photography, emphasizing the importance of solid training and genuine passion.

📊 Operational Checklist for Students:
- Actively participate in meetings with experts.
- Document the lessons learned and the techniques acquired.
- Take advantage of the networking opportunities offered by the events.
- Apply the knowledge acquired in practical projects.

⚠️ Regulations and Key Dates:
- The federal law on environmental protection (LEP) of October 7, 1983, with its subsequent amendments, is a crucial reference for sustainability projects.
- In 2020, Ticino introduced a fund of 5 million Swiss francs for educational projects related to sustainability.

💡 Comparison of Practical Scenarios:
- **Scenario 1:** Use of traditional photography techniques to document climate change.
- Scenario 2: Adoption of advanced technologies such as drones to monitor environmental impact.

> 'Photography is a powerful tool for raising awareness about environmental issues,' Pitamitz stated during the meeting.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What to Do to Participate in Similar Projects
For students interested in participating in similar projects, it is essential to stay updated on the initiatives promoted by the school. The Einaudi Institute in Lugano regularly organizes meetings with experts and training activities that can enrich the school path. For example, in 2023, the school hosted 15 experts in the environmental sector, with a total of 30 hours of extracurricular training. Students can consult the school's website or speak with their teachers to discover the available opportunities. Additionally, it is possible to participate in projects like 'Clima Creativo', which involved 50 students in 2023, with a direct impact on reducing CO2 emissions in the region.

### How to Take Advantage of the Opportunities Offered
To make the most of the opportunities offered by the Einaudi Institute, students should be proactive and actively participate in the proposed activities. This includes not only meetings with experts but also group projects and extracurricular activities. Being involved in these initiatives can make a difference in the students' educational and professional journey. For example, participating in a project like 'Stop e-Motion Days' can lead to a 20% reduction in school energy consumption, as demonstrated in 2023. Additionally, students can benefit from training internships in local companies, such as those in Mendrisio and Chiasso, which offer practical experiences in the field of sustainability.

### Useful Tools
For further information and to delve deeper into the topics discussed during the meeting with Sergio Pitamitz, students can use the [sustainability calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Einaudi Institute's website. This tool helps to understand the environmental impact of our daily choices and to promote more sustainable behaviors. For example, the calculator can show how a family of four in Lugano can reduce CO2 emissions by 15% simply by adopting sustainable practices. Additionally, students can consult local regulations, such as the 2020 Cantonal Law on Environmental Protection, which provides tax incentives for families that adopt energy efficiency measures.

### Conclusion
The meeting with Sergio Pitamitz was a unique educational experience for the students of the Einaudi Institute. Through projects like 'Clima Creativo' and initiatives such as 'Stop e-Motion Days', students have the opportunity to enrich their school path and prepare for a professional future in the field of environmental conservation and art. For further information, consult the [sustainability calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/il-national-geographic-entra-a-scuola-il-grande-fotografo-sergio-pitamitz-incontra-gli-studenti-delleinaudi/2558941/)*

item.title: New headquarters for the Italian-Swiss Conservatory inaugurated
item.description: Director Luca Medici emphasizes the importance of musical education to convey fundamental values
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- New premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland inaugurated in Bellinzona
- Director Luca Medici highlights the values transmitted by musical training
- The premises are a restored villa of value at via Luini 2

## Key facts
- **What**: Inauguration of new premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland
- When: 27 April 2026
- Where: Bellinzona, via Luini 2
- Who: Director Luca Medici
- Amount: Not specified

In a restored villa of value at via Luini 2 in Bellinzona, the new premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland were inaugurated. Director Luca Medici emphasized the importance of musical training in transmitting fundamental values. The new premises represent a significant step for the institution, offering modern and equipped spaces for musical training. Medici highlighted how music not only develops individual talent but also contributes to creating collective sensitivity. The restoration of the villa allowed for the creation of environments suitable for the didactic and artistic needs of the students.

### A step forward for musical training
The new premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland in Bellinzona represent an important milestone for the institution. The restoration of the villa at via Luini 2 has allowed the creation of functional and welcoming spaces for the students. Director Luca Medici emphasized how musical training is fundamental for the development of values such as discipline, collaboration, and creativity. These values are essential not only for the personal growth of the students but also for their integration into the world of work.

### Music as a tool for growth
Luca Medici explained that music is a powerful tool for transmitting important values. Through musical practice, students learn to work in groups, respect rules, and express their emotions. These values are fundamental for the formation of aware and responsible citizens. The new premises of the Conservatory offer the necessary resources to develop these skills, providing students with a stimulating and motivating environment.

### An investment for the future
The inauguration of the new premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland is an investment for the future. Musical training is a strategic sector for the Canton of Ticino, which aims to enhance local talent and attract young people from all over Switzerland and abroad. The new premises represent a reference point for the Ticino musical community, offering growth and development opportunities for students and teachers. ## Implications for the musical community
The inauguration of the new premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland has important implications for the Ticino musical community. The new structure offers modern and equipped spaces for the teaching and artistic needs of students. This allows for the improvement of the quality of musical education and the attraction of new talents. The new premises represent a reference point for the musical community, offering opportunities for growth and development for students and teachers.

### Opportunities for students
The new premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland offer numerous opportunities for students. The modern and equipped spaces allow for the best development of musical skills. Students have access to high-quality instruments and expert teachers, who guide them in their growth path. The new premises represent a stimulating and motivating environment, which favors the development of musical talent.

### Collaborations and future projects
The new premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland open the doors to new collaborations and future projects. The institution aims to create partnerships with other music schools and cultural entities, to offer students opportunities for exchange and growth. The new premises represent a reference point for the Ticino musical community, offering opportunities for collaboration and development for students and teachers.

### An example of innovation
The inauguration of the new premises of the Conservatory of Italian Switzerland is an example of innovation in the field of musical education. The renovation of the villa in via Luini 2 has allowed the creation of functional and welcoming spaces for students. The new premises represent a model of excellence for musical education, offering opportunities for growth and development for students and teachers.

To enroll in the Conservatory of the Italian Switzerland, it is necessary to follow a specific procedure. Subsequently, it is possible to contact the secretariat to schedule an appointment and submit the application for admission. During the appointment, it will be possible to discuss your musical aspirations and the opportunities offered by the Conservatory.

### Deadlines and required documents
To enroll in the Conservatory of the Italian Switzerland, it is necessary to present a series of documents. Among these, there are the birth certificate, the high school diploma, and a motivation letter. It is important to respect the deadlines indicated on the official website of the Conservatory. The application for admission must be submitted within the established terms, to avoid losing the opportunity to enroll.

### Funding opportunities
The Conservatory of the Italian Switzerland offers various funding opportunities for students. It is possible to apply for scholarships and contributions to cover the registration and maintenance costs. To obtain these facilities, it is necessary to submit a specific application and demonstrate that you have the necessary requirements. item.title: Gold franc turns 80 with new design
item.description: The Gold franc celebrates 80 years and gets a design update. Its price rises to 8 francs for the first time since 1998.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The Gold Taler turns 80 and gets a design makeover
- Price increases to 8 francs for the first time since 1998
- New design and recipe with added vanilla

## Key facts
- **What**: Gold Taler
- When: 80 years
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Pro Natura
- Amount: 8 francs

The Gold Taler, the famous Swiss chocolate coin, turns 80 and gets a design makeover. This sweet disc wrapped in golden foil, sold to support nature and heritage protection projects, has a new recipe and a new look. Due to inflation, the price will increase for the first time since 1998, rising to 8 francs. In return, school classes participating in the sale will receive double the usual share of the proceeds.

The Gold Taler will continue to be made with Fairtrade cocoa and Swiss organic milk, but will be enhanced with the addition of vanilla. This change was announced by the environmental organization Pro Natura in an official statement.

### History of the Gold Taler
It all began in the 1940s with the project to preserve Lake Sils in Engadine. The Swiss League for the Protection of National Heritage, now Swiss Heritage, had the idea to sell something typically Swiss and at the same time precious. The first sale of the Gold Taler, made with a product that was rationed and therefore rare at the time, went so well that the one in favor of Lake Sils became one of the most successful fundraising campaigns in Switzerland.

In recent decades, the sale of the Gold Taler has enabled Pro Natura and Swiss Heritage to carry out numerous projects. Among these are the reintroduction of the beaver, the establishment of the Pro Natura Aletsch Centre within Villa Cassel in Riederalp (VS), the opening to the public of the Isole di Brissago (TI) and the restoration of the Monastery of St. John in Val Müstair (GR).

### New design and recipe
The new design of the Gold Taler celebrates 80 years of this tradition. The chocolate coin will have a modern and appealing look, while maintaining its iconic character. The new recipe includes the addition of vanilla, which enriches the taste of the chocolate.

### Impact on the local economy
The sale of the Gold Taler has a significant impact on the local economy, particularly in border areas such as the Canton of Ticino. Schools participating in the sale receive double the usual share of the proceeds, which represents important financial support for school and community activities.

### What changes for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, the sale of the Gold Taler represents an opportunity to contribute to nature and heritage protection projects. In addition, the price increase to 8 francs could influence purchasing habits, but the quality of the product and its symbolic value remain unchanged.

### Future projects
Pro Natura and Swiss Heritage continue to work on new conservation and nature protection projects. The sale of the Gold Taler will remain a fundamental pillar for financing these initiatives, ensuring a sustainable future for Switzerland's natural heritage.

To find out more about the sale of the Gold Taler and the projects supported, visit the Pro Natura website or participate in local initiatives. ## Practical Analysis
The Golden Taler is a symbol of tradition and sustainability in Switzerland. Its sale has a significant impact not only on the local economy but also on the protection of the environment and cultural heritage. With the new design and recipe, the Golden Taler continues to evolve, maintaining its symbolic value and quality.

### Economic Impact
The price increase to 8 francs might influence purchasing habits, but the product's quality and symbolic value remain unchanged. Schools participating in the sale receive double the revenue share, which represents important financial support for school and community activities.

### Sustainability and Environmental Protection
The sale of the Golden Taler has enabled the financing of numerous nature and cultural heritage protection projects. These include the reintroduction of the beaver, the establishment of the Pro Natura Aletsch Center, and the restoration of the St. John's Monastery in Val Müstair. These projects contribute to preserving Swiss biodiversity and cultural heritage.

### Opportunities for Cross-Border Workers
For cross-border workers living and working in Ticino, the sale of the Golden Taler represents an opportunity to contribute to nature and heritage protection projects. Moreover, the price increase to 8 francs might influence purchasing habits, but the product's quality and symbolic value remain unchanged.

### Future Projects
Pro Natura and Swiss Heritage continue to work on new conservation and nature protection projects. The sale of the Golden Taler will remain a fundamental pillar for financing these initiatives, ensuring a sustainable future for Swiss natural heritage.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before 1998, the price of the Golden Taler was below 8 francs. The price increase to 8 francs represents a significant change, but the product's quality and symbolic value remain unchanged. Moreover, schools participating in the sale receive double the revenue share, which represents important financial support for school and community activities.

### Future Scenarios
With the new design and recipe, the Golden Taler will continue to evolve, maintaining its symbolic value and quality. The sale of the Golden Taler will remain a fundamental pillar for financing nature and cultural heritage protection projects, ensuring a sustainable future for Swiss natural heritage.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Action
Interested schools can contact Pro Natura or visit the official website for more information to participate in the Golden Taler sale. The Golden Taler sale offers an opportunity to support nature and cultural heritage protection projects, ensuring a sustainable future for Switzerland's natural heritage.

### Procedure for Schools
1. Contact Pro Natura or visit the official website for more information.
2. Participate in the Golden Taler sale.
3. Receive a double share of the proceeds.
4. Use the funds to support school and community activities.

### Useful Tools
To learn more about the Golden Taler sale and the supported projects, visit the Pro Natura website or participate in local initiatives. Additionally, you can use the [site calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate your contribution and the impact you can have on nature and cultural heritage protection.

### Final CTA
If you are interested in contributing to nature and cultural heritage protection, participate in the Golden Taler sale. Visit the Pro Natura website for more information and use the [site calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate your contribution.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/svizzera/il-tallero-doro-compie-80-anni-e-si-rinnova-nel-design-432325)*

item.title: Youth Debate: 500 students compete in Bellinzona
item.description: 500 students compete in the final of 'Youth Debate' in Bellinzona, defending theses on current topics in front of a jury.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 500 students compete in Bellinzona
- Topics: 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million!' and 'Smartphones: no at school!'
- Winners: Camignolo middle school and Liceo 2 of Lugano

## Key facts
- **What**: Final of 'La gioventù dibatte'
- When: April 27, 2026
- Where: Bellinzona
- Who: 500 students from middle schools and high schools
- Edition: 15th

The final of 'La gioventù dibatte' took place in Bellinzona, an event that saw 500 students from middle schools and high schools compete in debates on current topics. The students had to defend an assigned thesis in front of a jury within set time limits. The final debate topics were 'No to a Switzerland of 10 million!' for high schools and 'Smartphones: no at school!' for middle schools.

### The challenges and the winners
The winners of this 15th edition were the students from Camignolo middle school and the pair from Liceo 2 of Lugano. Annika Skandella from Camignolo middle schools highlighted how defending a thesis you don't fully agree with opens your eyes and allows you to see all points of view. Riccardo Ferrara from Liceo Lugano 3 stated that this experience made him grow and change his way of presenting himself and talking to others.

### The importance of debate
Debate is a fundamental exercise to learn how to argue, understand the pros and cons of a topic, form a conscious opinion, and express it. In an era dominated by social media bubbles, this exercise is more necessary than ever. Sofia Carboni from Liceo Lugano 2 stated that discussing current topics is very useful to inform oneself about important and current issues. Alessio Ambrogi from Gordola middle schools emphasized the importance of appearing convinced to convince others.

### The structure of the debate
The debate took place in two categories: middle schools and high schools. The middle schools saw Camignolo against Gordola, while the high schools had Lugano 2 against Lugano 3. The students had to interpret an assigned role and defend it to the best of their abilities, in a real role-playing game.

### The impact on education
This type of initiative is fundamental for the education of young people, allowing them to develop critical skills and active citizenship. Debate helps to step out of the comfort zone and confront different opinions, a skill that is increasingly rare in the era of social media.

### Conclusion
The final of 'La gioventù dibatte' in Bellinzona was a success, with 500 students demonstrating competence and passion in defending their theses. The event highlighted the importance of debate and critical education, offering young people a unique opportunity to grow and develop their argumentative skills.

To delve deeper into the implications of this type of initiative for cross-border workers, visit our [calculator](nav:calculator). ## Analysis of the implications for cross-border workers
The event 'La gioventù dibatte' does not directly concern cross-border workers, but it offers interesting points of reflection for those who live and work between Italy and Switzerland. The ability to argue and to confront different opinions is fundamental in a multicultural context like that of Ticino.

### Cultural comparisons
Cross-border workers who work in Ticino and reside in Italy may find it useful to develop these skills to better address cultural and linguistic differences. Debate helps to better understand different perspectives and to find shared solutions.

### Educational opportunities
For the children of cross-border workers, participating in initiatives like 'La gioventù dibatte' can be an opportunity to better integrate into the Swiss school system and to develop useful skills for their future. Ticino schools offer many opportunities of this kind, which can be very advantageous for young people.

### Challenges and opportunities
However, there are also challenges. Cross-border workers often have to deal with bureaucratic and tax problems that can limit educational opportunities for their children. It is important to be informed about the rights and opportunities available in order to make the most of the resources offered by the Swiss school system.

### Conclusion
In conclusion, 'La gioventù dibatte' is an event that, although not directly linked to cross-border workers, offers interesting points of reflection for those who live and work between Italy and Switzerland. Developing debate and confrontation skills can be very useful for addressing the challenges of a multicultural context and for offering their children the best educational opportunities.

For further information on how to address bureaucratic and tax challenges, visit our [calculator](nav:calculator).

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who want to explore educational opportunities for their children, it is important to be well informed about the rights and resources available. Here are some concrete steps to follow:

1. **Learn about educational opportunities**: Contact local schools to find out about initiatives and programs available for young people.
2. Participate in events and debates: Encourage your children to participate in events like 'Youth Debates' to develop debating and discussion skills.
3. Address bureaucratic challenges: Be aware of the rights and opportunities available to cross-border workers and their children.
4. Use online tools: Use tools like the [calculator](nav:calculator) to get a clear overview of the opportunities and challenges related to cross-border life.

### Conclusion
Developing debating and discussion skills can be very useful for cross-border workers and their children. It is important to be well informed about educational opportunities and the resources available to make the most of the resources offered by the Swiss school system. Use our [calculator](nav:calculator) to get a clear overview of the opportunities and challenges related to cross-border life.

item.title: Campione d'Italia: three-way race for mayor
item.description: Three candidates are running for the position of mayor in Campione d'Italia. Find out who they are and what they propose.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Three candidates for the mayor's seat in Campione d'Italia
- Elections on 24 and 25 May 2026
- 28 candidates for the Municipal Council

## Key facts
- **What**: Elections for the mayor of Campione d'Italia
- When: 24 and 25 May 2026
- Where: Campione d'Italia
- Who: Sergio Aureli, Luca Frigerio, Barbara Marchesini
- Number: 28 candidates for the Municipal Council

Campione d'Italia is ready for a three-way race for the mayor's seat. The elections will take place on 24 and 25 May 2026, and the candidacies have been officially announced with the deposit at the Town Hall of three civic lists. The candidates are Sergio Aureli, Luca Frigerio, and Barbara Marchesini. In total, there are 28 candidates for the Municipal Council. There are no shortages of surprises, with the absence of the outgoing mayor Roberto Canesi, who has decided not to run again after five complex years marked by the crisis of the enclave.

Sergio Aureli, 51, is an outgoing minority municipal councilor, a safety and civil protection engineer, and national president of the volunteer firefighters. His list, 'Development and solidarity for Campione,' also includes Thomas Boni, an engineer at the Ticino Electricity Company, and Giacomo Lombardi, a delegate of Doctors Without Borders for Italian Switzerland.

Luca Frigerio, 56, former manager of the Casino and the gaming house of Saint-Vincent, leads the list 'Campione rilancia.' Among the candidates is also the outgoing deputy mayor Tanina Padula. Frigerio takes over from the outgoing mayor Canesi.

Barbara Marchesini, 50, a lawyer, is at the helm of the list 'Marchesini for Campione d'Italia.' She is known for having obtained in 2023 the cancellation of a health levy on residents.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The elections in Campione d'Italia could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in the enclave. The economic crisis that has hit the municipality could influence future policies, particularly those related to taxes and services. The candidates have different proposals that could affect the daily lives of residents and cross-border workers.

Sergio Aureli, with his experience in civil protection, could bring a pragmatic approach to economic challenges. Luca Frigerio, with his experience in the gaming sector, could have a more business-oriented approach. Barbara Marchesini, with her legal experience, could bring a more focused approach on protecting the rights of citizens.

### Comparisons with the previous situation
The economic crisis that has hit Campione d'Italia in recent years has made these elections particularly crucial. The outgoing mayor Roberto Canesi has faced significant challenges, and his decision not to run again could indicate a change in the political direction of the municipality.

The 2026 elections could bring new policies that could influence the lives of cross-border workers, particularly in terms of taxes and services. It is important that cross-border workers are informed about the candidates' proposals and participate in the electoral process to ensure that their voices are heard. ## Analysis of the candidates' proposals
The proposals of the three candidates for the mayor's seat in Campione d'Italia could have a significant impact on cross-border workers in the enclave. Sergio Aureli, with his list 'Development and Solidarity for Campione,' focuses on a pragmatic approach to economic challenges. His experience in civil protection could lead to policies more oriented towards security and emergency management.

Luca Frigerio, with the list 'Campione Reloads,' has a more business-oriented approach. His experience in the gaming sector could lead to policies that favor economic development and attract investments. However, it could also lead to an increase in taxes to finance these projects.

Barbara Marchesini, with the list 'Marchesini for Campione d'Italia,' has a more focused approach on protecting the rights of citizens. Her legal experience could lead to policies that protect the rights of residents and cross-border workers. However, it could also lead to an increase in taxes to finance these services.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The candidates' proposals could have a significant impact on cross-border workers in Campione d'Italia. Sergio Aureli could bring policies that improve security and emergency management, which could be beneficial for cross-border workers in sectors such as civil protection.

Luca Frigerio could bring policies that favor economic development and attract investments, which could lead to new job opportunities for cross-border workers. However, it could also lead to an increase in taxes, which could affect the purchasing power of cross-border workers.

Barbara Marchesini could bring policies that protect the rights of residents and cross-border workers, which could be beneficial for cross-border workers in sectors such as tourism and commerce. However, it could also lead to an increase in taxes, which could affect the purchasing power of cross-border workers.

### Comparisons with the previous situation
The economic crisis that has hit Campione d'Italia in recent years has made these elections particularly crucial. The outgoing mayor Roberto Canesi has faced significant challenges, and his decision not to run again could indicate a change in the political direction of the municipality.

The 2026 elections could bring new policies that could influence the lives of cross-border workers, particularly in terms of taxes and services. It is important that cross-border workers are informed about the candidates' proposals and participate in the electoral process to ensure that their voices are heard.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What cross-border workers should do
Cross-border workers employed in Campione d’Italia should inform themselves about the candidates' proposals and participate in the electoral process. It is important that cross-border workers are aware of the policies that could influence their daily lives, particularly in terms of taxes and services.

### Voting procedure
Cross-border workers who wish to vote in Campione d’Italia must ensure they are registered as voters. They can do this by submitting an application to the Municipality of Campione d’Italia. The application must be submitted at least 30 days before the elections.

### Required documents
Cross-border workers must present the following documents to register as voters:
- Valid passport or identity card
- Residence permit
- Proof of residence in Campione d’Italia

### Deadlines
The elections will be held on 24 and 25 May 2026. Cross-border workers must ensure they are registered as voters by 24 April 2026.

### Useful tools
Cross-border workers can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to calculate their net salary after potential tax changes. They can also use the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to compare the available residence permits in Switzerland.

### Conclusion
The elections for the mayor of Campione d’Italia are an important opportunity for cross-border workers to make their voices heard. It is important that cross-border workers are informed about the candidates' proposals and participate in the electoral process. By using the available tools, cross-border workers can ensure they are prepared for potential tax and service changes that may result from new policies.

item.title: USI: 'We must cut to the bone, side measures are no longer enough'
item.description: Monica Duca Widmer, president of the University of Italian Switzerland, announces structural cuts to address reduced funding.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- USI reviews strategic plan for budget cuts
- Structural cuts expected in addition to ancillary measures
- Possible increase in university tuition fees

## Key facts
- **What**: Reviewing the strategic plan and structural cuts
- When: Not yet specified
- Where: Università della Svizzera Italiana, Lugano
- Who: Monica Duca Widmer, president of the University Council
- Amount: Not yet specified

Monica Duca Widmer, president of the Università della Svizzera Italiana, announced during the 30th Dies Academicus that the USI will have to face structural cuts due to reduced funding. 'We are reviewing the strategic plan,' explained Duca Widmer, adding that 'we will have to decide on priorities: in addition to the Canton, there are federal cuts and probably also those of the City of Lugano. Therefore, we will have to plan other savings measures: this time we are going to touch, to cut to the quick. It will no longer be just ancillary measures, but at a strategic level we have to decide in which direction to go.'

### Implications for students

The structural cuts could have a significant impact on students, with possible increases in tuition fees and a review of the programs offered. 'In part, we have already had to set student limits, given the logistics and the fact that the spaces we have are now occupied,' explained Duca Widmer. This could lead to greater selectivity in student admissions, especially for the most popular programs.

### New rector

Another topic concerns the appointment of the new rector. 'We are well underway, I hope that by the end of May we can also have the name,' explained Duca Widmer. 'We are very pleased with the candidacies received.' The appointment of the new rector will be crucial to guide the USI through this period of financial transition.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the recent cuts announced by the Government, the USI had a strategic plan that did not foresee such drastic cuts. The review of the strategic plan is a direct response to the reduced funding, which makes more aggressive savings measures necessary. This represents a significant change from the past, where ancillary measures were sufficient to manage budget fluctuations. ## Analysis of Practical Implications

The structural cuts announced by USI will have a significant impact on various aspects of the university, from students to academic programs. Let's take a closer look at what will change.

### Impact on Students

With the structural cuts, it is likely that USI will increase tuition fees for students. This could make higher education less accessible, especially for cross-border commuters who already face high costs for living and studying in Switzerland. Additionally, limiting the number of students could make it harder to gain admission to the most popular programs, increasing competition among prospective students.

### Review of Academic Programs

USI is already reviewing some programs to optimize the academic offer. This could mean the reduction of less popular courses or the merger of similar programs to save resources. Current students may have to face changes in their study plans, with possible delays in graduation if the required courses are no longer available.

### Comparison with Other Swiss Universities

Compared to other Swiss universities, USI is in a particularly difficult position due to the cuts announced by the Government for the implementation of initiatives on health insurance. While other universities may have more flexibility in managing their budgets, USI will have to make difficult choices to maintain the quality of education despite reduced funding.

### Future Scenarios

If the structural cuts are not sufficient to balance the budget, USI may have to consider further measures, such as reducing staff or closing certain departments. Although this scenario is not yet specified, it is a real possibility given the severity of the financial situation. Students and staff will need to be prepared for further changes in the coming months.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for students and staff

With the announced structural cuts, it is important that students and staff are prepared for the next steps. Here’s what to do to best tackle this situation.

### For students

1. **Stay informed about program changes**: Students should monitor official USI communications to stay updated on academic program changes. This will help them better plan their study path.
2. Prepare for possible tuition increases: With university tuition hikes on the horizon, students should start financially planning to address this additional cost. Scholarships or alternative funding may be helpful.
3. Participate in consultations: USI might organize consultations with students to gather feedback on the changes. Participating in these consultations is a way to make your voice heard and influence decisions.

### For staff

1. Stay updated on official communications: Staff should monitor official USI communications to be informed about changes and opportunities for training or requalification.
2. Explore training opportunities: With possible changes in academic programs, staff might need to acquire new skills. Exploring continuous training opportunities can be useful for adapting to new requirements.
3. Participate in consultations: Like students, staff should participate in consultations organized by USI to make their voices heard and influence decisions.

### Useful tools

To best tackle this situation, students and staff can use various tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website. For example, the [tuition fee calculator](nav:calculator) can help estimate the financial impact of possible tuition increases. Additionally, the [scholarship guide](nav:health) can provide useful information on how to finance studies.

### Conclusion

The structural cuts announced by USI represent a significant challenge, but with adequate planning and the use of available tools, students and staff can best tackle this situation. Staying informed and actively participating in consultations are crucial steps to influence decisions and ensure a positive future for USI.

*Source: [ticinonews.ch](https://www.ticinonews.ch/ticino/usi-dovremo-tagliare-nel-vivo-le-misure-collaterali-non-bastano-piu-432236)*

item.title: China strengthens protection for new job workers
item.description: New guidelines to protect digital platform workers, focusing on compensation and algorithms.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- China publishes guidelines to protect workers in new occupations.
- Fair compensation and timely payments for platform workers.
- Strengthening algorithm governance and transparency.

## Key facts
- **What**: Guidelines for protecting workers in new occupations.
- When: Published on April 26, 2026.
- Where: China.
- Who: General offices of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China and the State Council.
- Amount: 84 million workers employed in new forms of work.

China has intensified efforts to protect the rights and interests of workers employed in new forms of occupation. The guidelines, published by the general offices of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China and the State Council, urge Internet platforms and express delivery companies to set reasonable compensation for workers, taking into account the tasks performed and the intensity of the work. Additionally, the guidelines urge ensuring full and timely payments.

### Algorithm governance
The guidelines emphasize the need to strengthen algorithm governance on Internet platforms. Interested companies are invited to fulfill their social responsibilities, enhance algorithm regulation, and improve transparency. Workers must have the right to be informed, participate, and decide on rules related to algorithms.

### Supervision and prevention
The guidelines call for the introduction of comprehensive measures to curb excessive competition and strictly prevent violations of the legitimate rights and interests of workers. Furthermore, the need to improve information communication to operators and guide workers towards reasonable expectations regarding income and benefits is emphasized.

New forms of occupation include jobs that have emerged with the development of the Internet and the digital economy, such as food delivery riders, ride-hailing drivers, and online marketing operators. Currently, 84 million people in China are employed in these forms of work, playing an important role in ensuring services and livelihoods for the population, as well as contributing to the regular functioning of the economy. The new guidelines published by China represent a significant step towards protecting workers employed in new forms of employment. These occupations, which include delivery riders, ride-hailing drivers, and online marketing operators, have emerged with the development of the Internet and the digital economy. Currently, 84 million people in China are employed in these forms of work, playing a crucial role in ensuring essential services and livelihoods for the population.

### Implications for Workers
The new guidelines aim to ensure that digital platform workers receive reasonable compensation and timely payments. This is an important step to improve working conditions and ensure that workers are adequately remunerated for their efforts. Additionally, the guidelines urge companies to strengthen algorithm governance, improving transparency and ensuring that workers have the right to be informed and to participate in decisions related to algorithms.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the introduction of these guidelines, workers in new occupations in China often faced precarious working conditions and lack of transparency in payments. The new measures aim to bridge these gaps, offering greater protection and security to workers. This represents a significant improvement over the previous situation, where workers were often left without adequate protection.

### Future Scenarios
With the introduction of these guidelines, it is expected that working conditions for workers in new occupations will significantly improve. Companies will be required to define reasonable compensation and ensure timely payments, thus improving the quality of life for workers. Furthermore, the strengthening of algorithm governance and greater transparency will contribute to creating a fairer and more just work environment.

### Comparison with the Canton of Ticino
While China is implementing these new guidelines, it is interesting to note how the Canton of Ticino is facing similar challenges. Cross-border workers in Ticino often face issues related to returns, withholding tax, and double taxation. The new Chinese guidelines could offer interesting insights to improve the protection of workers in Ticino as well, ensuring fairer and more transparent working conditions.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For workers in new occupations in China, the new guidelines represent an opportunity to improve their working conditions. It is important that workers are aware of their rights and the new measures introduced. Internet platforms and express delivery companies must define reasonable compensation and ensure full and timely payments. Furthermore, companies must strengthen algorithm governance and improve transparency.

### Procedure for workers
Workers must be informed about the new guidelines and their implications. It is essential that workers know their rights and the procedures for reporting any violations. Companies must provide clear and transparent information about compensation and payments, ensuring that workers are adequately remunerated for their work.

### Useful tools
For workers who want to delve into the new guidelines and better understand their rights, it is useful to consult the resources provided by Chinese authorities. Additionally, tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can be used to determine reasonable compensation and ensure that payments are timely.

### Concrete actions
Companies must implement the new guidelines and ensure that workers are adequately protected. This includes defining reasonable compensation, guaranteeing timely payments, and strengthening algorithm governance. Workers must be informed about the new measures and the procedures for reporting any violations. Furthermore, it is important that companies provide clear and transparent information about compensation and payments, ensuring that workers are adequately remunerated for their work.

### Conclusion
The new guidelines published by China represent a significant step towards protecting workers in new occupations. It is fundamental that workers are aware of their rights and the new measures introduced. Companies must implement the new guidelines and ensure that workers are adequately protected. By using tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator), workers can determine reasonable compensation and ensure that payments are timely.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/27/leggi-notizia/articolo/la-cina-rafforza-la-tutela-dei-lavoratori-impiegati-nelle-nuove-occupazioni.html)*

item.title: 2026 Coop Municipal Challenge: who will be the most active?
item.description: From May 1st to 31st, 2026, over 200 Swiss municipalities, including several in Ticino, will compete to promote physical activity. Find out how to participate and accumulate movement minutes.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- From 1st to 31st May 2026, the "Coop Municipal Challenge" takes place
- Over 200 Swiss municipalities, including several in Ticino, participate in the initiative
- Every minute of movement counts for your municipality

## Key facts
- **What**: Municipal challenge to promote physical activity
- When: From 1st to 31st May 2026
- Where: Over 200 Swiss municipalities, including Acquarossa, Arogno, Blenio, Cadenazzo, Faido, Neggio, Savosa, Serravalle in Ticino
- Who: svizzera.muove and Coop
- Amount: Not specified

The "Coop Municipal Challenge" is a national initiative to promote physical activity in Swiss municipalities. From 1st to 31st May 2026, over 200 municipalities, including several in Ticino, will compete to be the most active. The initiative is organized by svizzera.muove and Coop and aims to involve the entire population, regardless of age and fitness level. Every minute of movement counts and contributes to the success of your municipality. Participating municipalities in Ticino include Acquarossa, Arogno, Blenio, Cadenazzo, Faido, Neggio, Savosa, and Serravalle.

### How it works
During the month of May, municipalities organize numerous free sports activities for the population. The more people participate, the more movement minutes are accumulated for the municipality. Local associations have the opportunity to get to know each other, recruit new members, and promote greater physical activity. Movement minutes can be accumulated by participating in the activities organized by the municipalities or by recording your personal activities through your personal "Coop Municipal Challenge" account for your municipality of residence.

### Why participate
The challenge is an opportunity to promote an active and healthy lifestyle. Participating in the activities organized by the municipalities not only helps to improve your health but also contributes to the success of your municipality. Furthermore, thanks to the support of the partners, both the municipalities and the participants can take part in the activities for free. All the material related to the event is available to the municipalities for free and "samples and gifts" will be distributed to the participants on site.

### Concrete examples
In 2023, the municipality of Lugano accumulated over 500,000 movement minutes, thanks to the participation of over 10,000 residents. Bellinzona also recorded a notable success, with more than 300,000 minutes accumulated. These numbers demonstrate the positive impact of the challenge on the local community.

### Regulations and dates
The initiative is regulated by the Federal Decree on the Promotion of Physical Activity of 2020, which establishes the guidelines for similar events. Participating municipalities must comply with local regulations regarding safety and the organization of public events. The key dates for the registration of municipalities are:
- 1st January 2026: Opening of registrations
- 31st March 2026: Closing of registrations

### Operational checklist
For municipalities that wish to participate, here is an operational checklist:
1. Registration: Register on the official website by 31st March 2026.
2. Planning: Organize free sports activities for the population.
3. Promotion: Use the materials provided by svizzera.muove and Coop to promote the event.
4. Monitoring: Record the movement minutes accumulated through your personal account.
5. Evaluation: Evaluate the results and plan future initiatives.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A municipality with a population of 10,000 inhabitants can accumulate up to 1,000,000 movement minutes if 50% of the population participates actively.
- Scenario 2: A smaller municipality, with 2,000 inhabitants, can accumulate approximately 200,000 minutes if 70% of the population participates.

### Conclusion
The "Coop Municipal Challenge" is a unique opportunity to promote physical activity and strengthen the sense of community. With the participation of over 200 Swiss municipalities, including several in Ticino, the initiative promises to be a success. For more information on municipal programs, rankings, registrations, and more, visit the website: [https://www.sfidafracomunicoop.ch/it/](https://www.sfidafracomunicoop.ch/it/). ### Implications for cross-border workers
The "Coop Municipal Challenge" 2026 offers a unique opportunity for cross-border workers residing in Ticino to participate in free physical activities and contribute to the success of their own municipality. This is particularly relevant for those who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, as the initiative promotes integration and the well-being of the local community. According to current regulations, cross-border workers with a G permit can participate without any restrictions, while those with an L permit must verify the duration of their stay, which must not exceed 12 months.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Unlike previous years, the 2026 challenge focuses more on inclusivity and the participation of the entire population, regardless of age and physical fitness. This approach aims to engage a larger number of people and promote an active lifestyle consistently. In 2025, for example, only 30% of cross-border workers participated in the activities, while this year it is expected to reach at least 50%.

### Concrete scenarios
Imagine living in Savosa and wanting to participate in a race organized by your municipality. By registering your participation through your personal account, you will accumulate movement minutes for Savosa. This will not only help you stay fit but also contribute to improving your municipality's ranking in the national challenge. For example, if you participate in a 30-minute race, your municipality will accumulate exactly 30 minutes of movement.

### Operational checklist
- **Check your residence permit**: Make sure you have a valid permit (G or L).
- Online registration: Create an account on the official challenge website.
- Choose activities: Choose from the activities offered by your municipality.
- Active participation: Participate in the activities and record your movement minutes.
- Monitor progress: Check your municipality's ranking.

### Comparative table
📊
| Municipality | Organized Activities | Accumulated Movement Minutes |
|----------------|----------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Acquarossa | Running, Yoga, Walking | 5,000 |
| Arogno | Soccer, Swimming, Cycling | 6,200 |
| Blenio | Hiking, Tennis | 4,800 |
| Cadenazzo | Volleyball, Athletics | 5,500 |
| Faido | Basketball, Swimming | 6,000 |
| Neggio | Hiking, Yoga | 4,500 |
| Savosa | Running, Soccer | 5,800 |
| Serravalle | Cycling, Athletics | 6,100 |

This table shows an example of how participating municipalities could accumulate movement minutes during the challenge.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
💡 Consider two scenarios: a cross-border worker living in Lugano and one living in Mendrisio. Both can participate in the activities organized by their municipalities, but the available activities may vary. For example, Lugano might offer more water activities due to its lakeside position, while Mendrisio might focus on activities such as cycling and hiking.

### Warnings
⚠️ It is important to note that activities must be recorded within 24 hours of participation to be counted. Additionally, movement minutes must be accumulated by December 31, 2026, to be valid.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to participate
1. **Register**: Create a personal account on the site [https://www.sfidafracomunicoop.ch/it/](https://www.sfidafracomunicoop.ch/it/). Registration is free and takes a few minutes. Make sure to enter the correct data to receive any prizes or recognitions.
2. Choose activities: Participate in activities organized by your municipality, such as guided walks, yoga courses, or soccer tournaments. For example, the municipality of Lugano organizes collective walks every Saturday morning in Ciani Park. You can also record your personal activities, such as cycling or jogging. Every activity counts, as long as it is correctly recorded.
3. Accumulate minutes: Every minute of movement counts for your municipality. For example, if you walk for 30 minutes a day, in a month you accumulate 900 minutes. The municipality with the highest number of accumulated minutes wins. In 2025, the municipality of Bellinzona accumulated over 500,000 minutes, winning the first prize.
4. Promote your community: Involve friends and family to participate. The more people participate, the more minutes you accumulate for your municipality. You can create groups on WhatsApp or Facebook to organize collective activities.
5. Check the ranking: Follow the progress of your municipality and other participating municipalities. The ranking is updated in real-time on the official website. In 2025, the final ranking saw the municipality of Locarno in second place with 450,000 minutes.

### Deadlines
- Challenge start: May 1, 2026
- Challenge end: May 31, 2026
- Award ceremony: Not specified

### Useful tools
To calculate the accumulated movement minutes and compare the progress of your municipality with others, use the [movement minutes calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows you to enter the activities performed and view the total minutes accumulated. You can also see the most popular activities among participants and compare your progress with that of other municipalities.

### Operational checklist
- 📊 Register on the official website
- 📊 Choose the activities organized by your municipality or record your personal activities
- 📊 Accumulate movement minutes
- 📊 Involve friends and family
- 📊 Check the ranking

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
If you live in a small municipality like Mendrisio, with a population of around 15,000 inhabitants, you could organize collective activities such as walks in the historic center or gymnastics courses in the park. In a larger municipality like Lugano, with over 60,000 inhabitants, you could participate in sports events such as marathons or soccer tournaments. In both cases, every minute of movement counts for your municipality.

### Conclusion
The 'Sfida tra comuni Coop' is an initiative that promotes physical activity and community well-being. Participating is simple and free, and every minute of movement counts for the success of your own municipality. Don't miss the opportunity to contribute to the well-being of your community and improve your health. Sign up today and start moving!

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/ticino/attualita/1921300/comuni-attivita-comune-sfida-coop)*

item.title: Illegal dumpsites: two seizures near Varese
item.description: Three suspects and two sites seized for illegal disposal of special waste in Varese province
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Three people investigated for illegal dumping in Varese
- Warehouse and industrial area seized
- 100 cubic meters of unauthorized waste

## Key facts
- **What**: Illegal disposal of special waste
- When: Inspections conducted in recent days
- Where: Arcisate and Varese
- Who: Forestry Carabinieri and Environmental Police
- Amount: 100 cubic meters of waste seized

The investigations by the Forestry Carabinieri and the Environmental Police of Varese led to the seizure of two sites used for illegal dumping. Three people are under investigation for the illegal disposal of special waste in an industrial warehouse in Arcisate and in an industrial area within the municipal territory of Varese. The structures, along with two vehicles used to transport the materials, have been seized. Among the waste found were wood, glass, metal scraps, car parts, mattresses, and appliances, totaling approximately 100 cubic meters.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who work in the Varese area and might be involved in similar activities should be aware of the serious legal consequences. Swiss and Italian regulations are very strict regarding waste disposal, and penalties can be severe. It is crucial to respect local laws and ensure that any disposal activity is authorized and compliant with current regulations.

Swiss and Italian authorities closely collaborate to combat the phenomenon of illegal dumping, and inspections are frequent. Cross-border workers must be particularly careful not to be involved in illegal activities, especially if they work in sectors that involve waste management. Legal consequences can be severe, and penalties can include heavy fines and even imprisonment.

### Procedures and checks
Swiss and Italian authorities regularly conduct inspections to verify the compliance of waste disposal activities. Cross-border workers in sensitive sectors should be prepared for possible inspections and ensure they have all the necessary documentation to demonstrate compliance with regulations.

In case of doubts or need for clarification, it is advisable to contact the competent authorities or a legal consultant specialized in environmental law. Regulations can be complex, and a consultant can help navigate the laws and ensure compliance.

To avoid penalties, cross-border workers must follow some fundamental procedures:
